# GeoTap API

> Access 71 US federal environmental and infrastructure data layers through a single API.

GeoTap aggregates real-time data from FEMA, USGS, EPA, NOAA, USDA/NRCS, USFWS, USACE, DOT, and Census Bureau into a unified REST API and MCP server for AI tools.

## Quick Start

For any US address or coordinate, start with:
- `GET /api/v1/query-address?address=123+Main+St,+Houston+TX` — geocode + environmental lookup in one call
- `GET /api/v1/spatial/point?lat=29.76&lng=-95.36&layers=flood_zones,wetlands` — point query with specific layers

## API Base URL

https://geotapdata.com/api/v1

## Authentication

Include `X-API-Key` header. Get a free API key at https://geotapdata.com/developers (one-click signup).

## MCP Server

Install: `npx geotap-mcp-server`
npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/geotap-mcp-server

## Core Endpoints

### Location Lookup (start here)
- `GET /query-address` — Address lookup + environmental data (flood zones, wetlands, soils, contamination, habitat)
- `GET /spatial/point` — Same as above with lat/lng coordinates
- `GET /geocode` — Address to coordinates only

### Spatial Queries
- `POST /spatial/in-polygon` — Query area with GeoJSON polygon (accepts lat/lng for auto-bbox)
- `GET /spatial/near` — Query radius around a point
- `GET /spatial/bbox` — Query bounding box
- `POST /spatial/summary` — Feature counts only (fast, small response)

### Rainfall (NOAA Atlas 14)
- `GET /rainfall/atlas14` — Precipitation frequency estimates
- `GET /rainfall/atlas14/idf` — IDF curves
- `POST /rainfall/hyetograph` — Design storm hyetograph
- `GET /rainfall/recommend` — Recommended SCS distribution for location
- `POST /rainfall/climate/project` — Climate change projections

### Watershed & Hydrology
- `POST /watershed/delineate` — Delineate watershed from pour point
- `GET /watershed/characteristics` — Basin characteristics
- `GET /watershed/flow-statistics` — Peak/low flow estimates
- `POST /hydrology/analyze` — Full hydrologic analysis (CN, Tc, peak Q)

### Curve Numbers
- `GET /cn/lookup` — Single CN by land use + soil type
- `POST /cn/analyze` — Weighted CN for polygon using NLCD + SSURGO

### Water Quality (EPA ATTAINS)
- `POST /water-quality/receiving-water` — Impairments + receiving water
- `GET /water-quality/impairments` — Quick lookup by HUC-12

### Elevation (USGS 3DEP)
- `GET /spatial/dem-stats` — Elevation statistics for bbox
- `GET /spatial/contours` — Contour lines
- `POST /spatial/dem-export` — DEM GeoTIFF export

### Stream Gages (USGS NWIS)
- `GET /gage-intelligence/{siteId}/summary` — Gage overview
- `GET /gage-intelligence/{siteId}/flood-frequency` — Bulletin 17C analysis
- `GET /gage-intelligence/{siteId}/flow-duration` — Flow duration curve
- `GET /gage-intelligence/{siteId}/storm-events` — Detected storm events

### Site Analysis
- `POST /site-analysis/report` — Comprehensive environmental assessment
- `POST /constraints/report` — Constraint mapping + developable area
- `POST /site-developability/report` — Developability scoring (0-100)

### Export
- `POST /export` — GeoJSON, Shapefile, CSV, KML, GeoPackage
- `GET /export/{jobId}` — Check export status

## Data Sources

| Agency | Data |
|--------|------|
| FEMA | Flood zones, FIRM panels |
| USGS | Elevation (3DEP), land cover (NLCD), hydrography (NHD), stream gages (NWIS) |
| EPA | Water quality (ATTAINS), Superfund, brownfields, TRI, NPDES |
| NOAA | Rainfall (Atlas 14), climate projections (CMIP6), tide/weather stations |
| USDA/NRCS | Soils (SSURGO), curve numbers (TR-55) |
| USFWS | Wetlands (NWI), critical habitat, endangered species |
| USACE | Waterway permits (Section 404) |
| DOT | National Bridge Inventory |
| Census | Demographics, TIGER boundaries, geocoding |

## Tips for AI Agents

1. **Start with `query_address`** — handles 90% of location-based questions in one call
2. **Always set `geometry="none"`** on spatial queries unless coordinates are specifically needed
3. **Specify layers** — querying all 37 layers returns huge responses; pick what you need
4. **Use lat/lng** — POST endpoints accept flat `lat` + `lng` params, auto-converted to GeoJSON
5. **Poll async jobs** — site analysis, constraints, and exports return jobIds; poll the status endpoint
6. **Check `_summary`** — every response includes a plain-English summary in `_summary`
7. **Check `_meta.sources`** — cite the federal agency, not just "GeoTap"
